name: Bring Your Own Beamer
description:
**Short description**

BYOB

**Full description**

A one-night-only happening audio-visual jam for visual artists and musicians to showcase work in an immersive format

Bring Your Own Beamer is a one-night-only happening for visual artists and musicians to showcase their work. BYOBs are immersive, spontaneous, and always surprising!

Bring Your Own Beamer 
 Bring Your Own Beamer 
 Audio-visual Jam 
 
 We're excited to announce that the Open Call for Bring Your Own Beamer (BYOB) has concluded, and now we're inviting you to view the results! 
 
 Bring Your Own Beamer is a one-night-only happening for visual artists and musicians to showcase their work. BYOBs are immersive, spontaneous, and always surprising! 
 
 Each participant brings their own projector or installation to share works in video, animation, VJ sets, mapping, AR, or other experimental formats. 
 
 There are no curators, no selection process, and no judges. This isn't an exhibition; it's a visual playground. It's a chance to showcase your experiments, meet other artists, and become part of one massive, collaborative light installation. 
 
 BYOB is an international movement launched by Rafaël Rozendaal. Since 2010, it has taken place in cities such as Berlin, New York, Tokyo, São Paulo, London, and Amsterdam. 
 
 Where : Gabriel Sundukyan National Academic Theatre 
 When : November 7 
 Time : 18:00-23:00 
 
 
 
 Partners 
 Partners 
 
 Misak Samokatian 
 
 The upcoming edition in Yerevan is initiated by Misak Samokatian — artist, musician, and art director working with light, sound, and spatial installations. 
 
 Misak Samokatian is an artist, musician, and art director. He has been involved in the field of interactive, kinetic, and audio-visual installations since 2011. 
 
 As an artist, he works with light, sound, video, space, and time. His works have been presented at True \ False Festival (USA), Burning Man Russia(RU), Synthspoium(RU, AM), Winzavod Centre for contemporary art(RU), Moscow Biennale of Contemporary Art(RU), Proun Moscow(RU), Urvakan Festival(AM), Hay-Art Cultural Centre(AM), NPAK(AM)
